assertions.description: fetch() is exposed in window context.
Request constructor is exposed; the retry / keepalive options live on the RequestInit dictionary it accepts.
Constructing a Request with {keepalive: true} reflects back as request.keepalive === true.
Constructing a Request with a `retry` option dictionary should not throw — Chrome 139 expands RequestInit with a retry knob for keepalive fetches.
assertions.test: (()=>{try{const r=new Request('/', {keepalive:true,retry:{maxAttempts:3}});return r instanceof Request;}catch(e){return false;}})()

assertions.description: request.headers is exposed; the keepalive+retry pipeline still uses the standard Headers object.
navigator.sendBeacon is still exposed; fetch+keepalive+retry is the modern replacement but sendBeacon remains for now.
